title: fritillaria pyrenaica subsp. boissieri
intro: Fritillaria pyrenaica subsp. boissieri, a subspecies of the Fritillaria pyrenaica plant, is a type of flowering bulbous perennial native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Fritillaria pyrenaica subsp. boissieri include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tinctures, decoctions, or infusions, although its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
